INSTR_RULES:
PLEASE READ ALL RULES BEFORE STARTING, AND REFER TO THE SUPPLEMENTARY DOCUMENT IF YOU NEED A REFRESHER. 

 - First, determine: "Is this article about a crime?" If No, skip all subsequent questions and move onto the next article. If yes, continue answering the rest of the questions. 

 - For the "Is this crime violent question" : If the crime is violent in nature (anything causing bodily harm to another human, such as murder or rape), select yes. If the crime is not violent (Does not cause bodily harm, such as theft or fraud), click no 

 - Identify the names of the criminal and victim. Write the names if provided. If there is no name provided, say "not provided". If there are multiple names provided, write each one down while using commas to separate them. 

 - If race or gender is explicitly stated, use that and set confidence to 100%. Otherwise, use the name to infer the race. If the name is also not provided, select the option "No name available" 

 - When not explicitly stated, you will guess race/gender from the name; provide an appropriate confidence percentage reflecting uncertainty.


 - If there are multiple perpetrators or multiple victims, separate names, races, genders, and confidence values with commas in the corresponding fields. for multiple choice questions, select multiple, and then use the provided textbox to list the races/genders/confidence values. 

 Race categories are as follows:

- white : corresponds to "White"

- black : corresponds to "Black or African American"

- hispanic : corresponds to "Hispanic or Latino (or Spanish origin)"

- asian : corresponds to "Asian"

- native : corresponds to "American Indian or Alaska Native"

- middle eastern : corresponds to "Middle Eastern or North African (MENA)"

- pacific islander : corresponds to "Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander"


Write your name at the bottom of this instruction form, then hit next to get started
